description: Mean DNS popularity rank across all mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower means more popular overall
Average of rankings
Country code top-level domain
Kurtosis of the issuer’s domain rank distribution on the observation date
Kurtosis of ranking
Cisco Umbrella DNS popularity rank for the domain on data_date (lower means more popular)
Composite attention metric approximately equal to the sum over domains of 1/rank^0.8 (alpha=0.8); reduces dominance of top ranks
Relative popularity calculated with alpha=0.8
Composite attention breadth score, approximately the sum over domains of 1/rank (alpha=1); higher indicates broader/stronger attention
Relative popularity calculated with alpha=1
Skewness of the issuer’s domain rank distribution on the observation date
Skewness of ranking
Rank threshold (cutpoint) below which the top 10% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer fall on the observation date; lower is more popular
The top 10% percentile ranking
Average rank among the top 10% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower values indicate stronger concentration of popularity at the top
The average ranking of the top 10% percentile
Rank threshold (cutpoint) for the top 25% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower is more popular
The top 25% percentile ranking
Average rank among the top 25% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower values indicate stronger popularity within that slice
The average ranking of the top 25% percentile
Rank threshold (cutpoint) for the top 50% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower is more popular
The top 50% percentile ranking
Average rank among the top 50% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower values indicate stronger popularity within that half
The average ranking of the top 50% percentile
Rank threshold (cutpoint) for the top 75% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower is more popular
The top 75% percentile ranking
Average rank among the top 75% best-ranked mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date; lower values indicate stronger popularity within that broad slice
The average ranking of the top 75% percentile
Best (lowest) DNS popularity rank among all mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date
The top ranking
Count of mapped domains with country-code TLDs (ccTLDs) for the issuer on the observation date; excludes generic TLDs like .com
Total number of country-code TLDs
Total count of mapped domains for the issuer on the observation date
Total number of sub-domains

For ongoing data it is wq_append_time, for history data it simulated from PIT simulation field in the MCD input settings. MCD builder can disable auto-filled value of wq_pit for ongoing, by checking the flag "Always simulate PIT" in MCD Output Config
